title: Thread Metadata
description: Schema for thread metadata.json files containing thread configuration and state

short_description.description: Brief description of the thread's purpose or content (optional)

thread_id.description: Unique identifier for the thread instance

user_public_key.description: Public key of the user who owns the thread

workflow_base_uri.description: Reference to the workflow that initiated this thread (e.g., 'sys:system/workflow/write-workflow.md'). Null for external/imported sessions that have no originating workflow.

session_id.description: Original session ID from the provider

session_path.description: Path to the original session file (optional)

imported_at.description: When this session was imported into the Base system

provider_metadata.description: Provider-specific metadata preserved from the original session

raw_data_saved.description: Whether the raw imported data has been saved

execution.description: Canonical execution attribution: where and how this thread's session ran. May be null for legacy threads that cannot be classified. See system/text/thread-execution-attribution.md.

mode.description: host = ran directly on the machine; container = ran inside a container

machine_id.description: machine_registry key for the host that ran the session; null only when no registry entry matches

container_runtime.description: Container runtime binary name; null for host mode

container_name.description: Container name; null for host mode. A container_name starting with 'base-user-' indicates per-user isolation.

inference_provider.description: Name of the AI provider being used (e.g., 'ollama', 'anthropic')

models.description: Array of model names used in the thread

tools_used.description: Array of tool names used during the session

bash_commands_used.description: Array of bash commands executed during the session

thread_state.description: Current lifecycle state of the thread

archived_at.description: Timestamp of archival (ISO 8601) - only present if thread_state is 'archived'

archive_reason.description: Reason for thread archival - 'completed' for successful completion, 'user_abandoned' for user-initiated abandonment

tools.description: Array of tools available to this thread

git_branch.description: Git branch name associated with this thread (format: 'thread/{thread_id}')

system_worktree_path.description: Path to the Git worktree for the system repository

user_worktree_path.description: Path to the Git worktree for the user repository

message_count.description: Total number of messages in this thread

tool_call_count.description: Total number of tool calls made in this thread

user_message_count.description: Total number of user messages in this thread

assistant_message_count.description: Total number of assistant messages in this thread

context_input_tokens.description: Uncached input tokens of the latest assistant turn — current context state, not a thread-lifetime sum. Combined with context_cache_creation_input_tokens and context_cache_read_input_tokens, this gives the current prompt size (what Claude CLI surfaces as '% context').

context_cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Cache-creation input tokens of the latest assistant turn. See context_input_tokens.

context_cache_read_input_tokens.description: Cache-read input tokens of the latest assistant turn. See context_input_tokens.

cumulative_input_tokens.description: Sum of input_tokens across every assistant turn in the thread. Used for cost accounting (each turn pays for its own input).

cumulative_output_tokens.description: Sum of output_tokens across every assistant turn in the thread. Used for cost accounting and generation totals.

cumulative_cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Sum of cache_creation_input_tokens across every assistant turn in the thread. Used for cost accounting (cache writes are billed per turn).

cumulative_cache_read_input_tokens.description: Sum of cache_read_input_tokens across every assistant turn in the thread. Used for cost accounting (cache reads are billed per turn).

tags.description: Array of categorization tags following base-uri format (e.g., 'user:tag/project-alpha')

tags_user_set.description: Whether tags were manually set by the user. When true, automatic tag analysis will skip this thread.

tags_analyzed_at.description: Timestamp when automatic tag analysis was last performed (ISO 8601). Prevents duplicate analysis runs.

public_read.description: Whether this thread can be read by anyone without authentication. Only affects read access - write operations are always limited to owner.

relations.description: Array of entity relations in format 'relation_type [[base_uri]]' (e.g., 'accesses [[user:task/my-task.md]]')

relations_analyzed_at.description: Timestamp when relation analysis was last performed (ISO 8601). Prevents duplicate analysis runs.

file_references.description: Array of file base_uris accessed during the thread session, extracted from timeline analysis

directory_references.description: Array of directory base_uris accessed during the thread session, extracted from timeline analysis

relations_cleanup_at.description: Timestamp when relation cleanup was last performed (ISO 8601)

has_continuation_prompt.description: True when the thread's assistant output contains at least one continuation prompt (see libs-server/metadata/continuation-signal.mjs for the signal vocabulary). Used by analyze-thread-relations to prefilter candidate source threads without reading their timelines.

continuation_prompt_count.description: Number of distinct continuation prompts in the thread's assistant output (counted fenced blocks plus standalone Continuation: line prefixes). See libs-server/metadata/continuation-signal.mjs.

session_status.description: Current session lifecycle status for web-client-initiated sessions

prompt_snippet.description: First 200 characters of the user prompt for display in the sessions panel

job_id.description: BullMQ job ID for queue correlation with the session creation job

model.description: Single canonical model identifier; legacy field carried alongside the models array on older imports.

tags_prompt_version.description: Version identifier of the tag-analysis prompt used when tags were last assigned.

tags_taxonomy_hash.description: Hash of the tag taxonomy snapshot used at analysis time, for cache invalidation.

tags_reasoning.description: Free-text rationale for tag assignments produced by the analysis pipeline.

title_prompt_version.description: Version identifier of the title-generation prompt used when title was last set.

visibility_analyzed_at.description: Timestamp when public_read visibility analysis was last performed.

review_needed.description: Marker set by data-migration tooling when an entity could not be classified automatically and needs manual review.
